title: How to Set Up a React + Vite Project in 2025 (The Right Way)
description: A modern React + Vite setup guide covering project structure, ESLint, path aliases, environment variables, and deployment to Cloudflare Pages.
author: MyAppAI Team
body:
Vite has become the standard build tool for React projects in 2025. It's faster than Create React App, more flexible than Next.js for pure SPAs, and pairs perfectly with Cloudflare Pages for deployment. Here's how to set up a production-ready React + Vite project from scratch.

## 1. Create the Project

```bash
npm create vite@latest my-app -- --template react
cd my-app
npm install
```

## 2. Project Structure

A clean structure for a medium-sized app:

```
my-app/
  src/
    components/     # Reusable UI components
    pages/          # Route-level page components
    hooks/          # Custom React hooks
    lib/            # Utilities and helpers
    styles/         # Global CSS
    App.jsx
    main.jsx
  public/
  index.html
  vite.config.js
```

## 3. Add React Router

```bash
npm install react-router-dom
```

Update `src/main.jsx`:

```jsx
import React from 'react'
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'
import { BrowserRouter } from 'react-router-dom'
import App from './App.jsx'
import './styles/app.css'

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <BrowserRouter>
      <App />
    </BrowserRouter>
  </React.StrictMode>
)
```

## 4. Configure Path Aliases

In `vite.config.js`:

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import path from 'path'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [react()],
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'),
      '@components': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/components'),
      '@pages': path.resolve(__dirname, './src/pages'),
    }
  }
})
```

Now you can import with `import Button from '@components/Button'` instead of relative paths.

## 5. Environment Variables

Vite uses `.env` files. Variables must be prefixed with `VITE_` to be accessible in the browser:

```bash
# .env.local
VITE_API_URL=https://api.example.com
VITE_ENABLE_ANALYTICS=true
```

Access them in code:

```javascript
const apiUrl = import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL
```

## 6. ESLint Setup

```bash
npm install -D eslint @eslint/js eslint-plugin-react eslint-plugin-react-hooks
```

Create `eslint.config.js`:

```javascript
import js from '@eslint/js'
import reactPlugin from 'eslint-plugin-react'
import reactHooks from 'eslint-plugin-react-hooks'

export default [
  js.configs.recommended,
  {
    plugins: { react: reactPlugin, 'react-hooks': reactHooks },
    rules: {
      'react-hooks/rules-of-hooks': 'error',
      'react-hooks/exhaustive-deps': 'warn',
    }
  }
]
```

## 7. Deploy to Cloudflare Pages

Add `wrangler.toml`:

```toml
name = "my-app"
pages_build_output_dir = "dist"
```

Deploy:

```bash
npm run build
npx wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name=my-app --branch=main
```

## 8. Handle Client-Side Routing

For SPAs with React Router, add a `public/_redirects` file so Cloudflare serves `index.html` for all routes:

```
/* /index.html 200
```

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- **Don't use CRA in 2025** â€” it's unmaintained and slow
- **Don't commit `.env.local`** â€” add it to `.gitignore`
- **Don't skip the `_redirects` file** â€” your routes will 404 on refresh
- **Don't use default exports everywhere** â€” named exports are easier to refactor

This setup gives you a fast, modern React app that's ready to deploy in minutes.
